title: penstemon heterodoxus
intro: Penstemon heterodoxus, also known as Hairy Beardtongue, is a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. Native to the southwestern United States, it is a perennial herb that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating coughs, sore throats, and respiratory issues. The plant is rich in salicylic acid and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, contributing to its health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating pain. Herbal preparations of Penstemon heterodoxus can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support immune function and relieve symptoms of colds and flu.
